reference: I have stage four colon cancer that has spread to my liver. The irinotecan and Avastin cocktail has shrunk the tumors significantly. I have been on this regimen since March two thousand and ten; however, lately, two porta hepatic lymph nodes in my mesentery have been swelling. My carcinoembryonic antigen levels are six point eight. I am fifty-four years old, in good health otherwise, and recover from my treatments well. I have my infusion every two weeks. Should this be a great concern and, if so, why? Thank you, T.S.
text: I have stage four colon cancer that has spread to my liver. The Irinotecan and Avastin cocktail has shrunk the tumors significantly. I have been on this regimen since March 2010. However, lately, two portahepatic lymph nodes in my mesentery have been swelling. My carcinoembryonic antigen levels are 6.8. I am 54 years old, in good health otherwise, and recover from my treatments well. I have my infusion every two weeks. Should this be a great concern, and if so, why? Thank you, TS.
